calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys' Fees And To Amend Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-17-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on plaintiff Adbrite, Inc.'s Motion for Attorney Fees and to Amend the Judgment.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Beldon Yee, #2647.

John V. Komar, Esq. appeared for plaintiff AdBrite, Inc.
No appearance by defendants Jack9 Entertainment, Inc., or YHVH, Inc.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, plaintiff's motion for attorney fees and to the Judgment is GRANTED.
AdBrite, Inc. is awarded attorney fees in the amount of $13,812.68.
AdBrite is awarded post-judgment interest in the amount of $2,2362.50.

New Judgment Total - $98,316.01.

Order to Amend the Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-18-2009 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2009 Continued To Aug-28-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Jul-16-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

John Komar, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Adbrite, Inc.
John Shapiro, Vice President and General Counsel for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants Jack9 Entertainment, Inc. or YHVH, Inc.
Defaults was entered on April 10, 2009. Amended default were entered on April 16, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on July 2, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Adbrite, Inc., and against the Defaulted Defendants Jack9 Entertainment, Inc. and YHVH, Inc. as follows:

Damages/Principal - $61,177.88.
Prejudgment Interest - $20,255.87.
Costs - $707.18, for a total judgment in the amount of $82,140.93.

Counsel for plaintiff shall submit a proposed judgment to the Court no later than July 21, 2009.
